reasoning:

For the first resolution date (2025-11-02), the expected seasonal mean is close to 10°C, so the probability of exceeding the target threshold (9.575°C) is slightly above 50%.
As we extend our horizon to 2025-11-25 and 2026-01-24, the climatological expected temperature steeply drops. While uncertainty in the raw weather pattern increases over time (which tends to draw predictions slightly toward 0.5 compared to a theoretical Z-score calculation), the dominant seasonal pull of winter significantly lowers the base probability of encountering a ~9.6°C day. I assign a 29% chance for late November and a 17% chance for late January, acknowledging both the seasonal plunge in temperatures and the potential for unseasonable warm anomalies (as suggested by rising global trends and anomalous peak winter temperatures).
